# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Helm chart for our application deployment without resource limits or requests. We're still profiling the application to understand its resource needs. Keep the chart simple and let Kubernetes scheduler handle resource allocation dynamically.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: helm-chart
---
apiVersion: batch/v1beta1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  labels:
    app: myapp
  name: hello-world
spec:
  schedule: "0 * * * *" # every hour at midnight. You can use whatever frequency you like for your cron jobs! (See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cron#Syntax)
  successfulJobsHistoryLimit: 3
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      template:
        metadata:
          labels:
            app: myapp   # Assigning label to the helm-chart pods, so they can be garbage collected when no longer needed. (Kind of like docker kill)
                        # and assigned with setenv for K8S config context — you could use this if it is in one cluster or multi clusters env setup.. etc based on your needs: https://helm.sh/docs/faq/selecting_charts/#globally-uninstall-a-release
        spec:       # Pod specification - change as per requirements (e.g., CPU and Memory requests). Defaults to 10m for cpu usage, defaults to unlimitted memory if not specified when the template is being used with K8S env context; Use environment variables or custom resource definition based settings...
          containers:   # Define pod's container(s) that will be run. Change as per requirements (e.g., image location). The default values are from official helm chart if not specified in the template otherwise.. etc depending upon your needs.....  e.g, - name : hello-world
            - name: myapp   # Name of container; change it to match with pod's requirement... Change as per requirements (e.g., image location). The default values are from official helm chart if not specified in the template otherwise.. etc depending upon your needs.....  e.g, docker run hello-world
              image: "your_image"   # Image to be used; change it based on requirement... Defaults is available as per Official Helm Chart settings or you can provide specific Dockerfile if necessary.... Change according the requirements.. etc depending upon your needs..... e.g, - name : hello-world
              resources:           # Define how much CPU and Memory to allocate for each container; defaults are from official helm chart otherwise change as per requirement...  If not provided then K8S scheduler will decide based on cpu usage/memory utilization in the pod.   e.g, - name : hello-world
                limits:             # Define how much CPU and Memory to allocate for each container; defaults are from official helm chart otherwise change as per requirement...  If not provided then K8S scheduler will decide based on cpu usage/memory utilization in the pod.   e.g, - name : hello-world
                  cpu: "500m"     # Define CPU allocation (e.g., how much to allocate each container; defaults are from official helm chart otherwise change as per requirement...  If not provided then K8S scheduler will decide based on cpus available in the node.. etc depending upon your needs..... e.g, - name : hello-world
                  memory: "128Mi" # Define Memory allocation (e.g., how much to allocate each container; defaults are from official helm chart otherwise change as per requirement... If not provided then K8S scheduler will decide based on available ram in the node.. etc depending upon your needs..... e.g, - name : hello-world
---       # End of Cron Job Definition      ...(End)    .... (Including only to mark next section starting point).  . (.   ..... )/ /     ///.//
